task_name: FLS Training Overview - Multiple Tasks Shown

frame_analyses.description: Grendel Medical logo and branding introduction
Dr. Glyn Burtt introduction discussing FLS exam
Task 1 peg transfer setup shown with red and yellow objects on pegs
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS peg transfer configuration visible

frame_analyses.description: Task 2 pattern cutting setup with gauze and marked circle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard 4cm diameter circle pattern visible on suspended gauze

frame_analyses.description: Task 3 endoloop setup with foam appendage and ligating loop
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard endoloop configuration with target marking visible

frame_analyses.description: Tasks 4 and 5 suturing setup with Penrose drain and suture materials
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard suturing configuration showing marked targets on drain

frame_analyses.description: Peg transfer being performed with graspers in motion
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstration of bimanual coordination and object transfer

frame_analyses.description: Virtual reality simulation interface showing laparoscopic training
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Gaming interface with timing and accuracy metrics displayed

confidence_rationale: Clear instructional content with product demonstration and educational narration. No actual FLS task performance to score.
technique_summary: Instructional video introducing FLS training concepts and demonstrating virtual reality training platform as alternative to traditional box trainer practice.

verbal_cues: peg manipulation and bimanual dexterity test
cutting out a shape accurately test
extracorporeal knot tight and loop ligation test
suturing with intracorporeal knotting is the biggest differentiator between people who take the test
critical lead up to the suturing test as it demands good hand eye coordination and bimanual dexterity
more fun way to practise the tasks
common_errors_addressed: inadequate preparation for intracorporeal knotting challenges
